citations.narrative:
On March 3,2026 a search inspection was conducted by APHIS officials. It was determined that the facility currently
exhibits goats, parrots, rabbits, pigs, sheep, and an alpaca without a valid USDA license. The business was previously
visited by USDA personnel in August of 2023, and informed that a license is required to exhibit these animals. A valid
license is necessary to protect the health and well-being of the animals and to comply with the Animal Welfare Act and
Regulations. The facility needs to obtain a USDA license or discontinue regulated activities.
No regulated activities may be conducted until a USDA license is obtained.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
Additional Inspectors:
KRISTIN NAPOLI, SUPERVISORY ANIMAL CARE SPECIALIST n

narrative:
2.1(a)(1) 
   
Requirements and application. 
On March 3,2026 a search inspection was conducted by APHIS officials. It was determined that the facility currently 
exhibits goats, parrots, rabbits, pigs, sheep, and an alpaca without a valid USDA license. The business was previously 
visited by USDA personnel in August of 2023, and informed that a license is required to exhibit these animals. A valid 
license is necessary to protect the health and well-being of the animals and to comply with the Animal Welfare Act and 
Regulations. The facility needs to obtain a USDA license or discontinue regulated activities. 
 
No regulated activities may be conducted until a USDA license is obtained. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
KRISTIN NAPOLI, SUPERVISORY ANIMAL CARE SPECIALIST n
